verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a p24 family ER-Golgi resident TM protein involved in COPII/COPI vesicular trafficking. It cycles between ER and Golgi via intracellular transport vesicles; no documented plasma membrane targeting, surface pool, or ectodomain shedding. Luminal orientation of its single TM domain positions the ectodomain toward the ER/Golgi lumen, not the extracellular face. No cell-surface-directed therapeutics, cancer/cell-death ecto-display, activation-induced surfacing, or tissue-restricted surface localization documented.
